subject: 16 Lessons on nutrition and healthy lifestyle
body:

**
Hi Jeffrey,

Check out the new premium content on nutrition and healthy eating.

http://learni.st/users/jacobvp/boards/83893-controlling-the-appetite?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=board_image
Controlling the Appetite (http://learni.st/users/jacobvp/boards/83893-controlling-the-appetite?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=board_title)
BY Jacob Van Putten (http://learni.st/users/jacobvp?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=curator_name)

Our appetites are a complex play of hormonal signals, emotional and cognitive desires, and cultural biases. Learning to wield a healthy appetite is an essential tool to maintain a healthy body and mind.

BY Emily Dingmann (http://learni.st/users/emily.dingmann.7?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=curator_name)

Snacking can be healthy and satisfying! Choose snacks that have a good balance of protein (ideally 10 grams), fiber (ideally 5 grams) and under 200 calories so that they serve their purpose - holding you over until your next meal!

For those who follow a steady exercise regimen, knowing what to eat before and after workouts is critical to providing your body the fuel it needs to build and repair muscle.

Celiac Disease is a condition caused by an autoimmune response the protein gluten. While this has been understood for awhile in the medical community, it's still less acknowledged that there is a wide range of gluten sensitivity.

Our bodies can't produce Vitamin B12 on their own, and since it's typically found in meat, fish or dairy products, those on a vegan diet need to ensure they're getting it through supplements or fortified foods.

BY Emily Dingmann (http://learni.st/users/emily.dingmann.7?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=curator_name)

BY Jacob Van Putten (http://learni.st/users/jacobvp?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=curator_name)

When a bitter substance is recognized by bitter receptors on the tongue, a “bitter reflex” occurs, releasing the gastric hormone gastrin, which stimulates digestive function, over time strengthening the function of the digestive organs. Learn more here.

BY Jacob Van Putten (http://learni.st/users/jacobvp?utm_source=one_off&utm_medium=member_email&utm_campaign=new_premium_content&utm_content=curator_name)

Getting diagnosed with celiac disease can be confusing and stressful, but it doesn't have to be. Yes, some major changes are coming, but I'll show you how to handle them in stride.

Nonstick pans are extremely common and cheap, but they can release harmful toxins when heated above certain temperatures or when too scratched. Learn how to stock your kitchen with safe cookware.

body_html:
iOS App · Android App

Hi Jeffrey,

Check out the new premium content on nutrition and healthy eating.

Controlling the Appetite
BY Jacob Van Putten

Our appetites are a complex play of hormonal signals, emotional and cognitive desires, and cultural biases. Learning to wield a healthy appetite is an essential tool to maintain a healthy body and mind.

buy now $0.99
Foods Nutritionists Want You to Eat
BY Emily Dingmann

Nutritionists are experts when it comes to eating well. These are the foods they want you to eat for a healthy, well balanced diet. Include these foods in your daily meals.

buy now $0.99
Eat Less with a High Protein Breakfast
BY Emily Dingmann

A new study has shown that eating a breakfast high in protein will help you eat less throughout the day. Read on for the research and a slew of high-protein breakfast ideas!

buy now $0.99
10 Healthy Snack Ideas
BY Emily Dingmann

Snacking can be healthy and satisfying! Choose snacks that have a good balance of protein (ideally 10 grams), fiber (ideally 5 grams) and under 200 calories so that they serve their purpose - holding you over until your next meal!

buy now $0.99
Pre and Post-Workout Nutrition
BY Jacob Van Putten

For those who follow a steady exercise regimen, knowing what to eat before and after workouts is critical to providing your body the fuel it needs to build and repair muscle.

buy now $0.99
Healthy Alternatives for Food Allergies!
BY Emily Dingmann

Navigating food allergies can be tough. Here I outline alternatives for common food allergies. I tried to keep them healthy, within a similar category and easily packable for school meals. Remember to check labels well!

buy now $0.99
How to Grill Vegetables
BY Dulcie Wilcox

A grill is a great way to get big flavor out of our favorite summer veggies. Here are tips and tricks to do it right.

buy now $0.99
Understanding Celiac Disease
BY Jacob Van Putten

Celiac Disease is a condition caused by an autoimmune response the protein gluten. While this has been understood for awhile in the medical community, it's still less acknowledged that there is a wide range of gluten sensitivity.

buy now $0.99
How to Stay Healthy in College
BY Emily Dingmann

The college years are ones we look back on fondly, but aren't often our healthiest years. Take a few simple steps to staying (relatively!) healthy in college.

buy now $0.99
Vegan? Why You Need Vitamin B12
BY Emily Dingmann

Our bodies can't produce Vitamin B12 on their own, and since it's typically found in meat, fish or dairy products, those on a vegan diet need to ensure they're getting it through supplements or fortified foods.

buy now $0.99
Urinary Tract Infections: Natural Cures and Prevention
BY Jacob Van Putten

UTIs are the second most common type of infection in the US, and are often simply treated by an antibiotic course, leaving the body susceptible to future infections. Learn how to treat and prevent UTIs using foods and supplements.

buy now $0.99
Food Allergies: A Nutritionist Helps to Understand
BY Emily Dingmann

Food allergies are increasingly common and if you've got school aged kids, you know this! Let me help you understand more about what food allergies are, and what it's like for the children and families who live with them.

buy now $0.99
Bitters and Digestion
BY Jacob Van Putten

When a bitter substance is recognized by bitter receptors on the tongue, a “bitter reflex” occurs, releasing the gastric hormone gastrin, which stimulates digestive function, over time strengthening the function of the digestive organs. Learn more here.

buy now $0.99
Cooking Methods and Carcinogens
BY Jacob Van Putten

High temperature cause chemical reactions in amino acids and sugars that produce harmful carcinogens and mutagens. Learn which cooking methods and foods are most likely to produce harmful byproducts.

buy now $0.99
I Have Celiac Disease, Now What?
BY Emily Dingmann

Getting diagnosed with celiac disease can be confusing and stressful, but it doesn't have to be. Yes, some major changes are coming, but I'll show you how to handle them in stride.

buy now $0.99
Using Safe Cookware
BY Jacob Van Putten

Nonstick pans are extremely common and cheap, but they can release harmful toxins when heated above certain temperatures or when too scratched. Learn how to stock your kitchen with safe cookware.
